taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to skin and fascia in the text below.
textField:
The skin is the largest organ of the body, comprising the *epidermis* and *dermis*. The *epidermis* is *keratinised* *stratified* *squamous* epithelium. Its layers from deep to superficial are: stratum *basale*, stratum *spinosum*, stratum *granulosum*, stratum *lucidum* (thick skin only), and stratum *corneum*. *Melanocytes* in the basal layer produce *melanin* pigment.

The *dermis* has a superficial *papillary* layer with *dermal* *papillae* and a deeper *reticular* layer rich in *collagen*. *Langer's* lines indicate the predominant orientation of dermal collagen fibres. A *dermatome* is the skin area supplied by a single *spinal* nerve.

*Superficial* *fascia* (*subcutaneous* tissue) contains fat, cutaneous nerves, and superficial vessels. *Deep* *fascia* is a tough, inelastic membrane that wraps muscles, forming *compartments* and *retinacula*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review skin and fascia features and try again.
